You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Artist. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language description from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper: simple clause for a single triple
    def simple_clause(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        if p == "birthDate":
            return f"{s} was born on {o}"
        if p == "birthYear":
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p == "birthPlace":
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p == "deathDate":
            return f"{s} died on {o}"
        if p == "deathPlace":
            return f"{s} died in {o}"
        if p == "origin":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        if p == "genre":
            return f"{s} plays {o} music"
        if p == "instrument":
            return f"{s} plays the {o}"
        if p == "occupation":
            return f"{s} is a {o}"
        if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            return f"{s} has performed with {o}"
        if p == "recordLabel":
            return f"{s} is signed to {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsStartYear":
            return f"{s} started their career in {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsEndYear":
            return f"{s} ended their career in {o}"
        if p == "nationality":
            return f"{s} is {o}"
        if p == "capital":
            return f"The capital of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "country":
            return f"{s} is located in {o}"
        if p == "isPartOf":
            return f"{s} is part of {o}"
        if p in {"language", "officialLanguage"}:
            return f"The language of {s} is {o}"
        if p == "stylisticOrigin":
            return f"{s} originated from {o}"
        if p == "derivative":
            return f"{s} is derived from {o}"
        if p == "musicFusionGenre":
            return f"{s} fuses elements of {o}"
        if p == "musicSubgenre":
            return f"{s} is a subgenre of {o}"
        if p == "stylisticOrigin":
            return f"{s} originated from {o}"
        # fallback
        return f"{s} {p} {o}"

    # Group triples by subject for more fluent sentences
    triples_by_subj: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        triples_by_subj.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    sentences: list[str] = []

    for subj, subj_triples in triples_by_subj.items():
        clause_parts: list[str] = []

        for t in subj_triples:
            # Special handling for genre + stylistic origin / derivative relationships
            if t.predicate == "genre":
                base = f"{subj} plays {t.object} music"
                # look for a related triple where the genre object is the subject
                related = None
                for r in triples:
                    if r.subject == t.object and r.predicate in ("stylisticOrigin", "derivative"):
                        related = r
                        break
                if related:
                    if related.predicate == "stylisticOrigin":
                        base += f", which originated from {related.object}"
                    else:  # derivative
                        base += f", which is derived from {related.object}"
                clause_parts.append(base)
            else:
                clause_parts.append(simple_clause(t.subject, t.predicate, t.object))

        # Combine clauses for this subject
        if len(clause_parts) == 1:
            sentences.append(clause_parts[0] + ".")
        else:
            sentences.append(", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1] + ".")

    # Join all subject sentences into one paragraph
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
